project.name: coffee-five
project.description: Coffee Five — Juan Medina Specialty Coffee website. Next.js 14 + Shopify Storefront API + Tailwind CSS.

rules: Always read CLAUDE.md before starting any task — it contains the full project context, brand guidelines, and architecture decisions.
Read docs/MIGRATION.md before building homepage components — it maps the approved wireframe to the Next.js architecture.
Read docs/SHOPIFY.md before any Shopify API work — it has the exact queries, metafield structure, and cart patterns.
Read docs/DESIGN.md before any styling work — it has the color system, typography rules, and component specs.
Read docs/I18N.md before adding any hardcoded strings — all UI text must go through the i18n system.
The approved wireframe is at docs/wireframe-reference.html. Match its design, layout, and section flow.
This site is Spanish-first. Default language is 'es'. English is a toggle.
Light cream palette from wireframe: bg #FAFAF8, text #1A1A1A, accent #6B4C3B. Dark sections for roasted coffee + footer.
Fonts: DM Sans (body) + DM Mono (monospace/labels). Load via next/font/google.
Use Tailwind CSS for all styling. No CSS modules, no styled-components.
Mobile-first responsive design. Base styles first, then sm:, md:, lg: breakpoints.
Server Components by default. Only use 'use client' when necessary (interactivity, hooks, animations).
GSAP for scroll-driven animations: use hooks from src/lib/gsap.ts (useScrollReveal, useStaggerReveal, useHoverLift, useFloatingLogo).
Framer Motion for component transitions: use variants from src/lib/framer.ts (fadeVariants, slideRightVariants, carouselVariants, mobileNavVariants).
Rule: scroll position → GSAP. React state change → Framer Motion. Never mix them on the same interaction.
All Shopify API calls go through src/lib/shopify.ts. Never call the API directly from components.
Product metafields use the 'coffee' namespace. See docs/SHOPIFY.md for the full list.
Cart state is client-side. Store cart ID in a cookie. Checkout redirects to Shopify's hosted checkout.
Images use Next.js <Image> component. All images need explicit width/height.
Photography placeholders should be intentional design elements (dashed border, X pattern, label), not broken images.
Location badges on green coffee lots are critical: En Colombia (gold), En tránsito (blue), Landed in US (green).
The four business pillars are: Grow · Source · Lab · Logistics.
Green coffee section = B2B tone (data-forward, sourcing depth). Roasted coffee section = consumer tone (lifestyle, approachable).
Consulting services are Juan's differentiator: Asesoría a Fincas, Laboratorio, Formación.
